Subject: Re: NPAL:  A new Fortran 95 version of the RAM PE code

Brian et al.,

The (very) limited information that I have on the geo-acoustic parameters in the Philippine
Sea is from David Knobles:

*****
At the V-3 location I have obtained an approximate geo-acoustic profile from analysis of
transmission loss (from the 2007 data) and lofargrams from passing ships using the 1987 data.
Also I have checked with NAVO. The sediment is soft; with a sound speed ratio slightly less
than unity.  The effective sediment thickness at V-3 appears to be on the order of about 50
meters.  The TL analysis suggests attenuation values on the order of about 0.15 dB/m @ 1 kHz.
Since the sound speed ratio is less than unity the sound speed gradient is important.  Again
the TL analysis suggests values on the order of 1.75 1/s.  We see clear evidence of scattering
of the bottom interacting paths.

I think the geo-acoustic model above should allow for approximate TL values to be produced.
If your system depends in some manner on beam TL, then in my opinion the situation may be more
complicated due to the scattering.  Beyond RAP range the details of CZs and their depth
dependence is very sensitive to sensor location and the sound speed profile in the water
column at the time of your experiment.

*****

Location V-3 is not far from the center of the planned tomographic array.



Peter
